description: Fix new issues found in second review round

userStories.title: _early_replies memory leak fix
userStories.description: As a long-running agent, I want _early_replies to not grow unbounded so that memory doesn't leak over time.
acceptanceCriteria: _early_replies entries have a TTL or max size limit
Stale entries are cleaned up automatically
ask()/reply() still works correctly with the cleanup in place
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: client/agent.py _early_replies dict grows without bound. Add max size (e.g. 1000) or TTL (e.g. 60s) with periodic cleanup.

userStories.title: ask() polling with exponential backoff
userStories.description: As an agent, I want ask() polling fallback to use exponential backoff instead of 0.1s fixed interval so that it doesn't hammer the server with 300 requests per ask().
acceptanceCriteria: Polling interval starts at 0.5s and doubles each iteration up to a max (e.g. 5s)
Total number of HTTP requests for a 30s timeout is drastically reduced
ask() still resolves correctly when reply arrives via polling
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Token invalidation and auto re-registration
userStories.description: As an agent, I want automatic re-registration when my saved token is rejected (401) so that I don't get stuck with stale credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: When an API call returns 401, the SDK clears saved credentials and attempts re-registration
Re-registration uses the same broker_url and name
New credentials are saved to credential store
Agent continues working after re-registration
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently from_credentials() loads stale tokens with no recovery path

userStories.title: Agent.connect_or_register() convenience method
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want a single method that loads saved credentials or registers if none exist so that I don't need try/except boilerplate.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent.connect_or_register(broker_url, name, meta=None) classmethod exists
If saved credentials exist, loads them and returns Agent
If no saved credentials, registers and saves credentials, returns Agent
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently need try from_credentials() except ValueError then register(). Should be one call.

userStories.title: Daemon webhook httpx client reuse
userStories.description: As a daemon operator, I want the webhook delivery to reuse an httpx client so that TCP connections are pooled.
acceptanceCriteria: deliver_webhook() reuses a shared httpx.AsyncClient instead of creating one per message
Client is properly closed on daemon shutdown
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: daemon/webhook.py creates async with httpx.AsyncClient() per delivery
